tasks.description: Write landing page copy for a B2B SaaS product targeting mid-market sales teams
tasks.input: Write landing page copy for a CRM tool called 'DealFlow' that helps mid-market B2B sales teams (50-200 employees) automate pipeline management. The main value prop is reducing time spent on manual CRM updates by 80%, so reps can focus on selling. Target audience: VP of Sales and Sales Ops managers frustrated with low CRM adoption and inaccurate forecasting. Include a headline, body copy, and CTA. The landing page should drive free trial signups.

scoring.5: Copy directly addresses VP of Sales / Sales Ops pain points (low CRM adoption, inaccurate forecasting, manual updates); uses their language and references their specific role challenges
scoring.3: Copy addresses sales teams generally but lacks role-specific pain points or language
scoring.1: Generic business copy with no clear audience targeting
scoring.0: Copy is not relevant to the described audience

scoring.5: Clearly follows a recognized persuasion framework (AIDA, PAS, BAB, or 4Ps) with all stages present and well-executed; framework choice is appropriate for the audience and channel
scoring.3: Some framework structure visible but stages are incomplete or the framework is not well-matched to the context
scoring.1: No recognizable framework structure; copy is loosely organized
scoring.0: Disorganized content with no persuasive structure

scoring.5: Headline is specific, benefit-driven, creates curiosity or names a pain point; 6-12 words; would stop a VP of Sales from scrolling
scoring.3: Headline conveys the product benefit but is generic or could apply to any CRM
scoring.1: Headline is vague, feature-focused, or uses jargon without clarity
scoring.0: No headline or completely irrelevant headline

scoring.5: CTA is action + benefit format, includes risk reversal (e.g., 'no credit card'), creates appropriate urgency, and is clearly placed; drives toward free trial signup as specified
scoring.3: CTA is present and relevant but uses generic wording ('Sign Up', 'Learn More') without benefit language
scoring.1: CTA is weak, buried, or mismatched to the goal (e.g., 'Contact Us' when goal is free trial)

scoring.5: Copy includes at least one social proof element, addresses likely objections (price, integration, adoption), benefits outnumber features, and the 80% time-saving claim is positioned credibly
scoring.3: Some persuasive elements present but missing social proof or objection handling
scoring.1: Copy lists features without connecting to benefits; no proof or objection handling
scoring.0: Copy is not persuasive; reads like a product spec
